name: Scalloped Salmon or Trout
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Cold cooked salmon or trout
recipeInstructions:
Serves 8 as an appetizer, or 3-4 as a main course.
Trim the fennel in the usual way, scraping away fibrous threads with a
potato peeler, or remove and discard the outer layers if they are
tired-looking or tough. Chop the flesh into small chunks and toss in 1/2
oz melted butter. Half cover the pan and leave to cook gently for 10
minutes, shaking the pan occasionally.
Add the bacon, cut into snippets, increase heat and cook, stirring frequently,
for several minutes until the bacon is cooked and the fennel is steamed.
Season with a good squeeze of lemon and plenty of pepper, then set aside to cool.
Mix with the fish, which should be broken into large chunks.
Make a sauce with 1-1/2 oz each butter and flour, the milk and the stock,
simmer gently, half covered, for about 10 minutes. Season with scant 1 teaspoon
mustard, a little salt, and plenty of pepper.
Gently fold in the fish mixture and divide between 8 small scallop shells or
cocottes, or put it all into one large gratin dish. Cover with foil and reheat
in the oven immediately, or if preparing ahead, set aside in a cool place until
close to serving time. If the mixture is cold when it goes into the oven,
it will need about 25 minutes at 425 F (220 C) gas mark 7 to become thoroughly heated through.
Stand the dish on a pre-heated baking sheet to help speed things up.
When thoroughly hot, remove the foil and sprinkle the fish mixture generously
with freshly toasted or fried breadcrumbs immediately before serving.
